questions.question: Which property is used to center text horizontally within its container?
questions.explanation: The text-align: center property centers inline content (text, images) horizontally within a block-level container.

questions.question: How do you horizontally center a block element with a fixed width?
questions.explanation: Setting margin: 0 auto on a block element with a defined width centers it horizontally. The 'auto' value distributes the remaining space equally on left and right.

questions.question: Which flexbox property combination centers an item both horizontally and vertically?
questions.explanation: On a flex container, justify-content: center centers items along the main axis and align-items: center centers them along the cross axis, achieving full centering.

questions.question: To use flexbox centering, which property must first be set on the parent container?
questions.explanation: The parent container must have display: flex to activate flexbox layout. Only then do properties like justify-content and align-items take effect.

questions.question: Setting margin-left and margin-right to ___ horizontally centers a block element with a defined width.
questions.explanation: When both horizontal margins are set to auto, the browser distributes remaining space equally, centering the element within its parent.

questions.question: The flexbox property ___ controls alignment along the main axis (horizontal by default).
questions.explanation: justify-content aligns flex items along the main axis. Common values include center, flex-start, flex-end, space-between, and space-around.
